## script to analysis the poms (only the pilot data) b cells in more detail
```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import bbknn
```

```{python}
adata = sc.read_h5ad("poms.annotated.dan.h5ad")
```

## check the dataset
```{python}
list(adata.obs.columns)
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="basic_labels")
sc.pl.dotplot(adata, var_names=["IGHD", "IGHM", "CD27", "MME", "CR2", "CD24"], groupby="basic_labels")
```

## subset out the b cell clusters
```{python}
pom_b = adata[adata.obs["basic_labels"].isin(["Naive_B", "Memory_B", "Transitional_B"])]
pom_b
```

## checking counts and logcounts data looks normal
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["counts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="blue", ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title("counts")

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["logcounts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="green", ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("logcounts")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## save an oroginal counts layer just to be safe and then save counts as X, as this is what normalise will run on
```{python}
pom_b.layers["original_counts"] = pom_b.layers["counts"].copy()
pom_b
pom_b.X = pom_b.layers["counts"].copy()
```

## normalise and log10 the counts
```{python}
sc.pp.normalize_total(pom_b, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(pom_b)
pom_b.layers['logcounts'] = pom_b.X.copy()
```

## plot distributions again
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["counts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="blue", ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title("counts")

sns.histplot(pom_b.layers["logcounts"].sum(1), bins=100, color="green", ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title("logcounts")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## select hvgs, perform batch correction, cluster and create umap
```{python}
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(
    pom_b, 
    n_top_genes=2000, 
    flavor="cell_ranger", 
    batch_key='patient_nice', 
    layer = 'logcounts',
    subset = False
)
sc.pp.pca(pom_b)
neighbors_within_batch = 25 if pom_b.n_obs > 100000 else 3
bbknn.bbknn(pom_b, batch_key='patient_nice', neighbors_within_batch=neighbors_within_batch)
sc.pp.neighbors(pom_b, n_pcs=10)
sc.tl.umap(pom_b)
random_indices = np.random.permutation(pom_b.shape[0])

sc.pl.umap(pom_b[random_indices,:], color = 'patient_nice', s = 20)
```

```{python}
sc.pl.umap(pom_b, color = ["IGHD", "IGHM", "CD27", "SPN", "CD38", "CD5", "CCR10"])
```
